concepts.name: Abhibhüya
concepts.gloss: Abhibhüya is the state of inner conflict between the gunas (sattva, rajas, tamas) where each tries to displace or neutralize the others. It is the perpetual psychological battle within the human system as the qualities compete for dominance.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental–Psychic (the inner theater of contention) · Ontic mode: Process (the shifting dominance among the guṇas, moment to moment) · Valence: Neutral dynamic — witnessing it is the practice · Guṇa: all three in flux (a rajasic dynamic) · Scale: Individual (ādhyātmika) · Causal status: Mechanism (how one’s dominant mode rises and yields) · Key relation: The guṇa triad in motion — the dynamic relation among the three.
coordinate.guna: all three in flux (a rajasic dynamic)
coordinate.valence: Neutral dynamic — witnessing it is the practice
coordinate.ontic_mode: Process (the shifting dominance among the guṇas, moment to moment)
coordinate.key_relation: The guṇa triad in motion — the dynamic relation among the three.
coordinate.causal_status: Mechanism (how one’s dominant mode rises and yields)

concepts.name: Rajas Projects, Tamas Veils, Sattva Reveals
concepts.gloss: The three gunas each have specific functions in obstructing or revealing truth: Tamas veils (hides truth); Rajas projects (creates illusory appearance); Sattva reveals (clears the veiling). Liberation requires reducing tamas, channeling rajas, cultivating sattva, then transcending even sattva.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental (discernment of guṇa-function) · Ontic mode: Functional schema (the operations of the three guṇas) · Valence: Neutral — a discriminative tool · Guṇa: all three (each named by its function) · Scale: Individual, with cosmic ground · Causal status: Mechanism (the operational role of each guṇa) · Key relation: Summary schema of the guṇa triad; pairs with the veiling teaching.
coordinate.guna: all three (each named by its function)
coordinate.ontic_mode: Functional schema (the operations of the three guṇas)
coordinate.key_relation: Summary schema of the guṇa triad; pairs with the veiling teaching.
coordinate.causal_status: Mechanism (the operational role of each guṇa)

concepts.name: Three Gunas as Daily Veils
concepts.gloss: The Bhagavata’s teaching: the Divine is hidden behind three gunas which veil. In daily life: develop guna-awareness. Notice moment-to-moment: am I in rajas (restless, ambitious, agitated), tamas (lazy, confused, dull), or sattva (clear, calm, discerning)? Daily food choices, company, activities each strengthen one guna. Conscious choices shift guna-balance over time.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic (the veiling of the Self) · Ontic mode: Condition (the guṇas functioning as veils over awareness) · Valence: Affliction — as veils, to be seen through · Guṇa: all three (each veils in its own way) · Scale: Individual, with cosmic ground · Causal status: Mechanism of concealment · Key relation: Correspondence — the guṇas veil Brahman; complements the reveal-project-veil schema.
coordinate.guna: all three (each veils in its own way)
coordinate.valence: Affliction — as veils, to be seen through
coordinate.ontic_mode: Condition (the guṇas functioning as veils over awareness)
coordinate.key_relation: Correspondence — the guṇas veil Brahman; complements the reveal-project-veil schema.

concepts.name: Two Forms of Brahman
concepts.gloss: Brahman has two forms: gross/embodied (sat) and subtle/disembodied (asat); mortal and immortal; defined and undefined. The gross form is air, sky, sun, moon, fire, water, etc. The subtle form is beyond all this. The description of the indescribable is ‘neti-neti.’

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic (the Absolute with and without qualities) · Ontic mode: Metaphysical distinction (two modes of the one Absolute) · Valence: Neutral — a truth to be realized · Guṇa: spans both — Saguna (with guṇas), Nirguna (beyond guṇas) · Scale: Cosmic / transcendent (Ādhidaivika), realized individually · Causal status: Ultimate ground (Nirguna as substrate; Saguna as its qualified aspect) · Key relation: Complementary pair — the guṇas pertain to Saguna and are transcended in Nirguna.
coordinate.guna: spans both — Saguna (with guṇas), Nirguna (beyond guṇas)
coordinate.seat: Psychic (the Absolute with and without qualities)
coordinate.valence: Neutral — a truth to be realized
coordinate.ontic_mode: Metaphysical distinction (two modes of the one Absolute)
coordinate.key_relation: Complementary pair — the guṇas pertain to Saguna and are transcended in Nirguna.
coordinate.causal_status: Ultimate ground (Nirguna as substrate; Saguna as its qualified aspect)

concepts.name: Rajas
concepts.gloss: Rajas is the quality of activity, passion, desire, and motion. It is characterized by dynamism, ambition, attachment, and restlessness. Rajas is the force that drives action, creation, and engagement with the world.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital (drive / activity), pervading all layers · Ontic mode: Constituent mode (a guṇa) · Valence: Neutral mode — necessary fuel for action, binding when dominant · Guṇa: Rajas (by definition) · Scale: Individual (prakṛti), with cosmic ground · Causal status: Conditioning mode (projects; the impetus to act) · Key relation: One of the guṇa triad — antagonist-complement to Sattva and Tamas.
coordinate.valence: Neutral mode — necessary fuel for action, binding when dominant
coordinate.key_relation: One of the guṇa triad — antagonist-complement to Sattva and Tamas.
coordinate.causal_status: Conditioning mode (projects; the impetus to act)

concepts.name: Sattva
concepts.gloss: Sattva is the quality of purity, harmony, knowledge, and virtue. It is characterized by clarity, illumination, and a sense of balance. Sattva represents the highest of the three gunas and is associated with wisdom, virtue, and spiritual enlightenment.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental–Psychic (clarity / luminosity), pervading all layers · Ontic mode: Constituent mode (a guṇa — one of the three fundamental modes of prakṛti) · Valence: Neutral mode, relatively liberating when cultivated — yet itself a subtle bond to be transcended · Guṇa: Sattva (by definition) · Scale: Individual (mental-spiritual prakṛti), with cosmic ground · Causal status: Conditioning mode (reveals; colors perception toward clarity) · Key relation: One of the guṇa triad — antagonist-complement to Rajas and Tamas.
coordinate.valence: Neutral mode, relatively liberating when cultivated — yet itself a subtle bond to be transcended
coordinate.ontic_mode: Constituent mode (a guṇa — one of the three fundamental modes of prakṛti)
coordinate.key_relation: One of the guṇa triad — antagonist-complement to Rajas and Tamas.
coordinate.causal_status: Conditioning mode (reveals; colors perception toward clarity)

concepts.name: Tamas
concepts.gloss: Tamas is the quality of inertia, darkness, ignorance, and heaviness. It is characterized by dullness, confusion, destruction, and dissolution. Tamas represents the lowest of the three gunas and clouds the mind.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Physical (inertia / density), dulling the mind · Ontic mode: Constituent mode (a guṇa) · Valence: Neutral mode — provides rest and stability, obscuring when dominant · Guṇa: Tamas (by definition) · Scale: Individual (prakṛti), with cosmic ground · Causal status: Conditioning mode (veils; inertia and concealment) · Key relation: One of the guṇa triad — antagonist-complement to Sattva and Rajas.
coordinate.valence: Neutral mode — provides rest and stability, obscuring when dominant
coordinate.key_relation: One of the guṇa triad — antagonist-complement to Sattva and Rajas.

concepts.name: Bhaya
concepts.gloss: Bhaya is the contraction of consciousness in the face of perceived threat. It is the organism’s oldest intelligence—the capacity to recognize danger and mobilize accordingly. But Bhaya is not only primal. It extends from the visceral panic of the body to the cognitive anxiety of the planner and the existential dread of the philosopher. In the classical framework, it reveals the fragility of egoic identity: we fear because we identify with something that can be lost.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital (the tremor of fear) · Ontic mode: State (durable disposition / Sthāyibhāva) · Valence: Faculty — wholesome caution, afflictive as pervasive dread · Guṇa: Rajas–Tamas · Scale: Individual (ādhyātmika) · Causal status: Dispositional ground · Key relation: Ripens into Bhayānaka Rasa; fed by apprehension and panic.
coordinate.valence: Faculty — wholesome caution, afflictive as pervasive dread
coordinate.key_relation: Ripens into Bhayānaka Rasa; fed by apprehension and panic.

concepts.name: Hāsa
concepts.gloss: Hāsa is the lightening energy of consciousness—the force that lifts, releases, and reminds us that the structures we build around our lives are not as solid as they seem. It is not merely the response to a joke. Hāsa is the cognitive capacity to perceive incongruity, the emotional willingness to delight in absurdity, and the social grace of shared vulnerability that laughter creates. In the classical view, it arises when expectations are subverted in a non-threatening way, and its function is to dissolve rigidity—in the body, in thought patterns, and in the fortress of the ego.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital (the lightness of mirth) · Ontic mode: State (durable disposition / Sthāyibhāva) · Valence: Faculty — wholesome levity, deflective when armored · Guṇa: Sattva in equilibrium · Scale: Individual, with interpersonal expression · Causal status: Dispositional ground · Key relation: Ripens into Hāsya Rasa; fed by transient delight and surprise.
coordinate.valence: Faculty — wholesome levity, deflective when armored
coordinate.key_relation: Ripens into Hāsya Rasa; fed by transient delight and surprise.

concepts.name: Jugupsā
concepts.gloss: Jugupsā is the emotional recoil from what threatens bodily or moral integrity. It is the force that makes us turn away from decay, cruelty, and corruption—both physical and ethical. In its biological form, it protects against contamination. In its moral form, it serves as the foundation of ethical discernment, the visceral refusal to participate in what degrades. The classical tradition recognizes it as the root of both purity and prejudice, depending on whether it is guided by wisdom or by ignorance.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Physical–Vital (visceral revulsion) · Ontic mode: State (durable disposition / Sthāyibhāva) · Valence: Faculty — wholesome discernment, contempt when chronic · Guṇa: Sattva (as discernment)–Tamas (as contempt) · Scale: Individual, with a moral (Ādhibhautika) dimension · Causal status: Dispositional ground · Key relation: Ripens into Bībhatsa Rasa; fed by aversion.
coordinate.valence: Faculty — wholesome discernment, contempt when chronic
coordinate.key_relation: Ripens into Bībhatsa Rasa; fed by aversion.

concepts.name: Krodha
concepts.gloss: Krodha is the fire of the psyche—a high-energy response to perceived violation, injustice, obstruction, or betrayal. It is heat and movement, the emotional equivalent of a boundary drawn in the sand. In its dharmic form—kṣātra-krodha, the righteous anger of the warrior—it protects truth, defends the vulnerable, and refuses to tolerate what should not be tolerated. In its distorted forms, it burns indiscriminately, consuming the person who carries it as readily as it consumes its target.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital (the rising fire of anger) · Ontic mode: State (durable disposition / Sthāyibhāva) · Valence: Faculty tending to affliction (protective, poisonous when indulged) · Guṇa: Rajas-dominant · Scale: Interpersonal (Ādhibhautika) · Causal status: Dispositional ground (a standing cause) · Key relation: Ripens into Raudra Rasa; fed by the wave Amarṣa (indignation).
coordinate.valence: Faculty tending to affliction (protective, poisonous when indulged)
coordinate.key_relation: Ripens into Raudra Rasa; fed by the wave Amarṣa (indignation).

concepts.name: Rati
concepts.gloss: Rati is the centripetal force of the human heart—the gravitational pull toward beauty, connection, and union. It is not limited to romantic desire, though it certainly includes it. Rati is the soul’s fundamental “yes” to life: the warmth that draws a parent to a child’s sleeping face, the quiet pleasure of a garden coming into bloom, the devotional surrender of the bhakta before the divine. It is the emotional current that makes us reach toward what we find beautiful, and in that reaching, discover something essential about ourselves.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital (the heart’s pull toward beauty and union) · Ontic mode: State (durable disposition / Sthāyibhāva) · Valence: Faculty — wholesome as generous love, binding as attachment · Guṇa: Sattva in equilibrium, rajasic in excess · Scale: Interpersonal (Ādhibhautika) · Causal status: Dispositional ground (a durable emotional foundation) · Key relation: Ripens into Śṛṅgāra Rasa; fed by vyabhicāribhāvas such as longing and jealousy.
coordinate.guna: Sattva in equilibrium, rajasic in excess
coordinate.seat: Vital (the heart’s pull toward beauty and union)
coordinate.valence: Faculty — wholesome as generous love, binding as attachment
coordinate.key_relation: Ripens into Śṛṅgāra Rasa; fed by vyabhicāribhāvas such as longing and jealousy.

concepts.name: Śama
concepts.gloss: Śama is the ground of being—the abiding stillness beneath all emotional weather. Championed by Abhinavagupta as the ninth and foundational Sthāyībhāva, it is not the absence of feeling but the presence of a witnessing awareness that allows all other feelings to arise and dissolve without disturbance. It is the rasa of the sage, the Sthita-prajña of the Gītā, the condition in which consciousness rests in its own nature rather than being swept along by its contents.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic (the still peace beneath all feeling) · Ontic mode: State — the foundational Sthāyibhāva (Abhinavagupta’s ninth) · Valence: Virtue — the equanimity to be uncovered (not the withdrawal that counterfeits it) · Guṇa: Sattva, tending beyond the guṇas · Scale: Individual, opening to the transcendent · Causal status: Ground (the substratum to which the other states return) · Key relation: Ripens into Śānta Rasa; the still ground of the other eight sthāyibhāvas.
coordinate.seat: Psychic (the still peace beneath all feeling)
coordinate.valence: Virtue — the equanimity to be uncovered (not the withdrawal that counterfeits it)
coordinate.key_relation: Ripens into Śānta Rasa; the still ground of the other eight sthāyibhāvas.
coordinate.causal_status: Ground (the substratum to which the other states return)

concepts.name: Śoka
concepts.gloss: Śoka is the breaking open of the heart in the face of impermanence. It is the emotional acknowledgment that what we love can be taken, that what we build can crumble, that we ourselves are transient. Legend holds that the first poem—the Rāmāyaṇa—was born when the sage Vālmīki witnessed the killing of a bird and his raw grief spontaneously crystallized into rhythmic verse. The very word śloka (verse) derives from śoka (sorrow). In the classical view, grief is not merely something we suffer; it is something that creates—it gives birth to empathy, poetry, and ultimately to wisdom.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital (the weight of loss in the heart) · Ontic mode: State (durable disposition / Sthāyibhāva) · Valence: Faculty — wholesome as honest grief, afflictive when it becomes a home · Guṇa: Sattva–Tamas · Scale: Interpersonal (Ādhibhautika) · Causal status: Dispositional ground · Key relation: Ripens into Karuṇā Rasa; fed by dejection and anxiety.
coordinate.seat: Vital (the weight of loss in the heart)
coordinate.valence: Faculty — wholesome as honest grief, afflictive when it becomes a home
coordinate.key_relation: Ripens into Karuṇā Rasa; fed by dejection and anxiety.

concepts.name: Utsāha
concepts.gloss: Utsāha is the propulsive force of the psyche—the will to act, to overcome, to rise. It is not the shallow excitement of novelty but the deep, sustained energy that makes a person capable of enduring difficulty in service of something larger than themselves. In the classical schema, it is the emotional engine of the Vīra (heroic) Rasa—the quality that transforms an ordinary person into someone willing to meet the world’s demands with courage and stamina.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital (heroic drive and resolve) · Ontic mode: State (durable disposition / Sthāyibhāva) · Valence: Faculty — wholesome resolve, compulsive striving in excess · Guṇa: Rajas–Sattva · Scale: Individual (ādhyātmika) · Causal status: Dispositional ground · Key relation: Ripens into Vīra Rasa; fed by resolve and vigor.
coordinate.valence: Faculty — wholesome resolve, compulsive striving in excess
coordinate.key_relation: Ripens into Vīra Rasa; fed by resolve and vigor.

concepts.name: Vismaya
concepts.gloss: Vismaya is the opening of consciousness when reality exceeds its current frame. It is the emotion of the threshold—the moment when the known dissolves and something unexpected, vast, or incomprehensible appears. In children, it is the default setting. In adults, it is the door that keeps closing and must be deliberately reopened. The classical tradition regards Vismaya as the seed of both scientific inquiry and spiritual vision—the state in which the ego’s certainties crack open to admit the possibility of the truly Real.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental–Psychic (the opening to mystery) · Ontic mode: State (durable disposition / Sthāyibhāva) · Valence: Faculty — wholesome awe, sensation-seeking in excess · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual (ādhyātmika) · Causal status: Dispositional ground · Key relation: Ripens into Adbhuta Rasa; fed by astonishment.
coordinate.valence: Faculty — wholesome awe, sensation-seeking in excess
coordinate.key_relation: Ripens into Adbhuta Rasa; fed by astonishment.

concepts.name: Ālasya
concepts.gloss: A heavy disinclination to act. In measure it is restful ease; in excess it sinks into sloth and stagnation that becomes a way of life; in deficiency it is a compulsive inability to be still.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Physical–Vital · Ontic mode: Wave · Valence: Faculty — a rest signal; sloth in excess · Guṇa: Tamas-dominant · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Contributory feeder · Key relation: Dampens Utsāha; feeds the tamasic states.
coordinate.valence: Faculty — a rest signal; sloth in excess
coordinate.key_relation: Dampens Utsāha; feeds the tamasic states.

concepts.name: Amarṣa
concepts.gloss: The slow burn of indignation at a violated value — the transient wave that rises to fuel durable anger. In measure it is a clean signal that something is wrong; in excess it smoulders into resentment; in deficiency it is a failure to register genuine violation.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital · Ontic mode: Wave (transient episode / Vyabhicāribhāva) · Valence: Faculty — a passing signal of violated value, neither virtue nor vice in itself · Guṇa: Rajas-dominant · Scale: Individual, with interpersonal trigger · Causal status: Contributory cause (a feeder that nourishes a durable state) · Key relation: Part that feeds the whole — rises to fuel Krodha (Sthāyibhāva), ripening toward Raudra Rasa.
coordinate.valence: Faculty — a passing signal of violated value, neither virtue nor vice in itself
coordinate.key_relation: Part that feeds the whole — rises to fuel Krodha (Sthāyibhāva), ripening toward Raudra Rasa.
coordinate.causal_status: Contributory cause (a feeder that nourishes a durable state)

concepts.name: Apasmāra
concepts.gloss: A slipping-away of recollection and presence — a momentary blanking that unmoors the thread of experience. In its ordinary form it is simple absent-mindedness; in excess it is a disorienting loss of continuity; its opposite is an anxious over-holding of every detail.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental · Ontic mode: Wave · Valence: Faculty tending to affliction (the lost thread) · Guṇa: Tamas · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Contributory feeder · Key relation: Interrupts Smṛti; unsettles the durable emotions.
coordinate.valence: Faculty tending to affliction (the lost thread)
coordinate.key_relation: Interrupts Smṛti; unsettles the durable emotions.

concepts.name: Asūyā
concepts.gloss: The wound of comparison — resentment at another’s good fortune or worth. In measure it can spur healthy aspiration; in excess it sours affection and hardens into begrudging spite; in deficiency it can mask as a flattering refusal to discern real merit.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital · Ontic mode: Wave · Valence: Faculty tending to affliction (envious comparison) · Guṇa: Rajas–Tamas · Scale: Interpersonal (Ādhibhautika) · Causal status: Contributory feeder · Key relation: Sours Rati and feeds Krodha.
coordinate.key_relation: Sours Rati and feeds Krodha.

concepts.name: Autsukya
concepts.gloss: The sweet ache of longing — the soul’s memory of a wholeness it aches toward. In measure it is aspiration and anticipation; in excess it becomes restless craving that cannot rest in the present; in deficiency it is a flat absence of desire.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital · Ontic mode: Wave · Valence: Faculty — aspiration; craving in excess · Guṇa: Rajas–Sattva · Scale: Individual, with interpersonal object · Causal status: Contributory feeder · Key relation: Feeds Rati (toward Śṛṅgāra Rasa).
coordinate.key_relation: Feeds Rati (toward Śṛṅgāra Rasa).

concepts.name: Avahitthā
concepts.gloss: The masking of true feeling behind composure. In measure it is social tact and self-possession; in excess it becomes a self-alienating suppression that hides the heart even from oneself; its absence is an unfiltered over-exposure.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Mental · Ontic mode: Wave · Valence: Faculty — tact; suppression in excess · Guṇa: Rajas–Tamas · Scale: Interpersonal (Ādhibhautika) · Causal status: Contributory feeder · Key relation: Conceals the durable emotions from view.
coordinate.key_relation: Conceals the durable emotions from view.

concepts.name: Āvega
concepts.gloss: A sudden surge or shock that seizes the system. In emergency it is mobilizing; in excess it disorganizes into overwhelm and reactivity; its absence is a sluggish failure to respond when response is called for.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital · Ontic mode: Wave · Valence: Faculty — a mobilizing surge · Guṇa: Rajas-dominant · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Contributory feeder · Key relation: A sudden wave that colors many durable emotions.
coordinate.key_relation: A sudden wave that colors many durable emotions.

concepts.name: Capalatā
concepts.gloss: A restless inability to hold still. In measure it is quick responsiveness and spontaneity; in excess it is scattered volatility that starts everything and finishes nothing; its lack is a rigid immovability.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital · Ontic mode: Wave · Valence: Faculty — responsiveness; volatility in excess · Guṇa: Rajas-dominant · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Contributory feeder · Key relation: Destabilizes the durable emotions (paired in the text with Āvega).
coordinate.key_relation: Destabilizes the durable emotions (paired in the text with Āvega).

concepts.name: Cintā
concepts.gloss: Future-projected worry that loops over imagined outcomes. In measure it is useful foresight; in excess it becomes corrosive rumination that generates catastrophe; in deficiency it is a heedless refusal to anticipate at all.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental · Ontic mode: Wave · Valence: Faculty — a foresight signal · Guṇa: Rajas-dominant · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Contributory feeder · Key relation: Future-projected; feeds Bhaya and Śoka (paired in the text with Viṣāda).
coordinate.key_relation: Future-projected; feeds Bhaya and Śoka (paired in the text with Viṣāda).

concepts.name: Dainya
concepts.gloss: A collapse of self-worth into helplessness and lowliness. In its cleanest form it can soften into true humility; in excess it becomes corrosive self-abasement and victimhood; in deficiency it stiffens into a pride that cannot bow.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital · Ontic mode: Wave · Valence: Faculty tending to affliction (self-diminishment) · Guṇa: Tamas-dominant · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Contributory feeder · Key relation: Feeds Śoka; opposes Utsāha.
coordinate.key_relation: Feeds Śoka; opposes Utsāha.

concepts.name: Dhṛti
concepts.gloss: A steady holding-firm through difficulty and a settled contentment with what is. In equilibrium it is the ballast that keeps the emotional weather from capsizing; in excess it stiffens into stubbornness; in deficiency it is a resolve that crumbles at the first obstacle.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental–Vital · Ontic mode: Wave · Valence: Faculty — wholesome steadiness · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Contributory feeder (stabilizing) · Key relation: Steadies the durable emotions; allied to Buddhi and Mati.
coordinate.key_relation: Steadies the durable emotions; allied to Buddhi and Mati.

concepts.name: Garva
concepts.gloss: The fortress of self-regard. In its faint form it is healthy self-respect; in excess it inflates into pride that distorts perception and repels connection; in deficiency it collapses into self-effacement.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Mental · Ontic mode: Wave · Valence: Faculty tending to affliction (inflated self-regard) · Guṇa: Rajas–Tamas · Scale: Individual, with interpersonal display · Causal status: Contributory feeder · Key relation: Distorts the durable emotions; kin to Mada.
coordinate.key_relation: Distorts the durable emotions; kin to Mada.

concepts.name: Glāni
concepts.gloss: The felt exhaustion of body and spirit after exertion or grief. In measure it is an honest call to rest; in excess it becomes chronic collapse and lifelessness; its absence shows as a driven refusal to acknowledge one’s own limits.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Physical–Vital · Ontic mode: Wave · Valence: Faculty — a depletion signal · Guṇa: Tamas-dominant · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Contributory feeder · Key relation: Dampens the durable emotions; typically follows Śrama.
coordinate.key_relation: Dampens the durable emotions; typically follows Śrama.

concepts.name: Harṣa
concepts.gloss: The sunbreak of joy — an unbidden uprising of gladness. In equilibrium it is received and shared freely; in excess it inflates into a manic elation that cannot land; in deficiency it is a joyless flatness that cannot be gladdened.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital · Ontic mode: Wave · Valence: Faculty — a wholesome uprising of gladness · Guṇa: Sattva–Rajas · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Contributory feeder · Key relation: Nourishes Rati and Hāsa (toward Śṛṅgāra and Hāsya).
coordinate.valence: Faculty — a wholesome uprising of gladness
coordinate.key_relation: Nourishes Rati and Hāsa (toward Śṛṅgāra and Hāsya).

concepts.name: Jaḍatā
concepts.gloss: The paralysis of overwhelm — a protective freeze that suspends response. In measure it buys a moment before an unbearable stimulus; in excess it locks into numb immobility; in deficiency it is an inability to pause even when flooded.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Physical–Mental · Ontic mode: Wave · Valence: Faculty — a protective freeze · Guṇa: Tamas-dominant · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Contributory feeder · Key relation: Suspends the durable emotions.
coordinate.key_relation: Suspends the durable emotions.

concepts.name: Mada
concepts.gloss: The heady inflation of intoxication, wealth, or success. In small measure it is exuberant vitality; in excess it swells into arrogance that clouds judgment and courts a fall; its lack appears as an inability to enjoy one’s own good fortune.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Mental · Ontic mode: Wave · Valence: Faculty tending to affliction (inflation) · Guṇa: Rajas–Tamas · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Contributory feeder · Key relation: Feeds Garva; clouds the durable emotions.
coordinate.key_relation: Feeds Garva; clouds the durable emotions.

concepts.name: Maraṇa
concepts.gloss: The felt approach of death — the ultimate transition. As a lived state it reframes and gathers all the durable emotions; met consciously it can open into peace; fled from, it becomes terror; denied, it leaves life strangely unlived.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Physical–Psychic · Ontic mode: Wave (the terminal transition) · Valence: Faculty — the ultimate signal · Guṇa: Tamas, opening beyond the guṇas · Scale: Individual, with cosmic dimension · Causal status: Contributory feeder / terminus · Key relation: Gathers and reframes all the durable emotions (toward Karuṇā and Śānta).
coordinate.key_relation: Gathers and reframes all the durable emotions (toward Karuṇā and Śānta).

concepts.name: Mati
concepts.gloss: A settled clarity of judgment — the mind arriving at a firm, reasoned conclusion. In equilibrium it is wise decisiveness; in excess it stiffens into dogmatic certainty; in deficiency it is a chronic inability to reach any conclusion.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental · Ontic mode: Wave · Valence: Faculty — wholesome discernment · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Contributory feeder (stabilizing) · Key relation: Steadies the durable emotions; allied to Buddhi and Dhṛti; resolves Vitarka.
coordinate.key_relation: Steadies the durable emotions; allied to Buddhi and Dhṛti; resolves Vitarka.

concepts.name: Moha
concepts.gloss: A fog in which the map replaces the territory. In its mildest form it is ordinary uncertainty; in excess it is a clouded judgment that distorts every perception; its opposite is a brittle over-certainty that mistakes itself for clarity.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental · Ontic mode: Wave · Valence: Faculty tending to affliction (clouded judgment) · Guṇa: Tamas-dominant · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Contributory feeder / mechanism of concealment · Key relation: Distorts all the durable emotions; dispelled by Vibodha.
coordinate.key_relation: Distorts all the durable emotions; dispelled by Vibodha.

concepts.name: Nidrā
concepts.gloss: The descent toward sleep. In measure it is natural restoration answering the body’s need; in excess it dulls waking life into perpetual somnolence and avoidance; in deficiency it is a wired inability to let go into rest.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Physical–Vital · Ontic mode: Wave · Valence: Faculty — a restoration signal · Guṇa: Tamas-dominant · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Contributory feeder · Key relation: Dims all the durable emotions; the threshold to Supta.
coordinate.key_relation: Dims all the durable emotions; the threshold to Supta.

concepts.name: Nirveda
concepts.gloss: A quiet emptying of investment in the surface of life. In equilibrium it is clean sadness that opens into genuine inquiry; in excess it hardens into nihilism and contempt; in deficiency it is papered over by a brittle, reflexive positivity.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Mental · Ontic mode: Wave (transient episode / Vyabhicāribhāva) · Valence: Faculty — a signal of readiness for inquiry; affliction when it hardens into nihilism · Guṇa: Sattva (clean letting-go) / Tamas (despair) · Scale: Individual (ādhyātmika) · Causal status: Contributory feeder · Key relation: Ripens toward Śānta (dispassion) or, unrefined, into Karuṇā.
coordinate.valence: Faculty — a signal of readiness for inquiry; affliction when it hardens into nihilism
coordinate.key_relation: Ripens toward Śānta (dispassion) or, unrefined, into Karuṇā.

concepts.name: Śaṅkā
concepts.gloss: A wary contraction before a suspected threat. In equilibrium it is protective vigilance that scans and then relaxes; in excess it curdles into corrosive, generalized suspicion; in deficiency it is a naive blindness to real danger.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Mental · Ontic mode: Wave · Valence: Faculty — protective wariness · Guṇa: Rajas-dominant · Scale: Individual, with interpersonal trigger · Causal status: Contributory feeder · Key relation: Feeds Bhaya, ripening toward Bhayānaka Rasa; peaks as Trāsa.
coordinate.key_relation: Feeds Bhaya, ripening toward Bhayānaka Rasa; peaks as Trāsa.

concepts.name: Smṛti
concepts.gloss: The arrival of the relevant past into the present. In equilibrium it is a servant that supplies experience when needed; in excess it becomes rumination that will not release the past; in deficiency it is a forgetful unmooring from one’s own history.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental · Ontic mode: Wave · Valence: Faculty — wholesome recall · Guṇa: Sattva · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Contributory feeder · Key relation: Supplies the durable emotions with their remembered material; its failure is Apasmāra.
coordinate.key_relation: Supplies the durable emotions with their remembered material; its failure is Apasmāra.

concepts.name: Śrama
concepts.gloss: The honest tiredness that follows real effort. In equilibrium it is a satisfying fatigue that calls for restoration; in excess it is pushed past into exhaustion and burnout; in deficiency it is the restlessness of one who cannot let effort come to rest.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Physical–Vital · Ontic mode: Wave · Valence: Faculty — an exertion signal · Guṇa: Rajas, tipping to Tamas · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Contributory feeder · Key relation: Precedes Glāni and Ālasya.
coordinate.key_relation: Precedes Glāni and Ālasya.

concepts.name: Supta
concepts.gloss: The state of being fully asleep — the deep withdrawal the inner weather subsides into. In balance it is the restorative night from which one returns renewed; in excess it becomes escape into oblivion; its lack is a depleting sleeplessness.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Physical–Psychic · Ontic mode: Wave (a transient state of sleep) · Valence: Faculty — natural rest · Guṇa: Tamas · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Contributory feeder · Key relation: The suspension into which the durable emotions rest; ended by Vibodha.
coordinate.key_relation: The suspension into which the durable emotions rest; ended by Vibodha.

concepts.name: Trāsa
concepts.gloss: Sudden overwhelming fright — the acute spike of panic that seizes the whole system. In its function it is the alarm that saves in true danger; in excess it becomes disabling terror; its deficiency is a dangerous fearlessness before real threat.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital · Ontic mode: Wave · Valence: Faculty — acute alarm · Guṇa: Rajas-dominant · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Contributory feeder · Key relation: The acute peak of Śaṅkā; feeds Bhaya toward Bhayānaka Rasa.
coordinate.key_relation: The acute peak of Śaṅkā; feeds Bhaya toward Bhayānaka Rasa.

concepts.name: Ugratā
concepts.gloss: A fierce, harsh intensity. In measure it is the righteous force that meets genuine wrong; in excess it loses restraint and becomes cruelty; in deficiency it is a limpness that cannot summon necessary force.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital · Ontic mode: Wave · Valence: Faculty tending to affliction (harshness) · Guṇa: Rajas–Tamas · Scale: Interpersonal (Ādhibhautika) · Causal status: Contributory feeder · Key relation: Intensifies Krodha toward Raudra Rasa.
coordinate.key_relation: Intensifies Krodha toward Raudra Rasa.

concepts.name: Unmāda
concepts.gloss: The dissolving of the map — a disordering surge where ordinary coherence gives way. It is afflictive by nature; in its milder register it is a temporary loss of grip under extremity; recovered from, it can even leave a strange clarity in its wake.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental–Psychic · Ontic mode: Wave · Valence: Faculty — an extreme, disordering surge · Guṇa: Rajas–Tamas · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Contributory feeder · Key relation: Overwhelms the durable emotions.
coordinate.key_relation: Overwhelms the durable emotions.

concepts.name: Vibodha
concepts.gloss: The moment the fog lifts — a return to alertness from sleep or confusion, a small awakening. In equilibrium it is fresh clarity; in excess it can become a hypervigilant over-alertness; in deficiency it is a lingering grogginess that never fully arrives.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental–Psychic · Ontic mode: Wave · Valence: Faculty — a wholesome flash of clarity · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Contributory feeder · Key relation: Dispels Moha and Nidrā; lifts the durable emotions toward Śānta.
coordinate.valence: Faculty — a wholesome flash of clarity
coordinate.key_relation: Dispels Moha and Nidrā; lifts the durable emotions toward Śānta.

concepts.name: Viṣāda
concepts.gloss: Present-tense despair — a sinking heaviness when effort fails. Deeper and more static than anxious Cintā; in excess it becomes an immobilizing hopelessness; in deficiency it is a manic denial that cannot admit defeat.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Mental · Ontic mode: Wave · Valence: Faculty tending to affliction (despair) · Guṇa: Tamas-dominant · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Contributory feeder · Key relation: Feeds Śoka (toward Karuṇā); present-tense counterpart to Cintā.
coordinate.key_relation: Feeds Śoka (toward Karuṇā); present-tense counterpart to Cintā.

concepts.name: Vitarka
concepts.gloss: The back-and-forth of reasoning that weighs alternatives. In measure it is careful deliberation that arrives at judgment; in excess it becomes an endless argumentative churn that cannot rest in any choice; in deficiency it is a heedless failure to weigh at all.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental · Ontic mode: Wave · Valence: Faculty — deliberation; paralysis in excess · Guṇa: Rajas–Sattva · Scale: Individual · Causal status: Contributory feeder · Key relation: Precedes and is resolved by Mati; unchecked, it feeds Cintā.
coordinate.key_relation: Precedes and is resolved by Mati; unchecked, it feeds Cintā.

concepts.name: Vrīḍā
concepts.gloss: The contraction of being seen. In measure it is healthy conscience and modesty; in excess it becomes toxic self-erasure and hiding; in deficiency it is a shamelessness that has lost the inner check.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital · Ontic mode: Wave · Valence: Faculty — a conscience signal; toxic as self-erasure · Guṇa: Tamas-tinged (contraction) · Scale: Interpersonal (Ādhibhautika) · Causal status: Contributory feeder · Key relation: Modulates Rati, Bhaya, and Śoka.
coordinate.valence: Faculty — a conscience signal; toxic as self-erasure
coordinate.key_relation: Modulates Rati, Bhaya, and Śoka.

concepts.name: Vyādhi
concepts.gloss: The felt distress of illness — bodily suffering that colors mood and thought. In measure it humbles and clarifies what matters; in excess it defeats and consumes the whole field of experience; its denial is a reckless ignoring of the body’s signals.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Physical · Ontic mode: Wave · Valence: Faculty — a distress signal · Guṇa: Tamas-tinged · Scale: Individual, with environmental cause (Ādhibhautika) · Causal status: Contributory feeder · Key relation: Colors Śoka, Bhaya, and Glāni.
coordinate.key_relation: Colors Śoka, Bhaya, and Glāni.

concepts.name: Part I: Vāta
concepts.gloss: Its five subdoshas—Prāṇa (governing mind, senses, and vital breath), Udāna (governing speech, effort, and upward movement), Samāna (governing intestinal peristalsis and nutrient absorption), Apāna (governing elimination and downward movement), and Vyāna (governing circulation and peripheral sensation)—together orchestrate the entire kinetic life of the organism. On the psychological plane, Vāta is the energy of creativity, spontaneity, enthusiasm, and the restless hunger for new experience.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Physical–Vital (movement, the nervous system, prāṇa) · Ontic mode: Constituent — a doṣa (the bio-energetic principle of movement) · Valence: Neutral faculty (balance is the aim) · Guṇa: Rajas-dominant (movement, dynamism) · Scale: Individual — constitutional (prakṛti), current expression (vikṛti) varying · Causal status: Governing principle (governs all movement — circulation, breath, nerve impulse) · Key relation: One of the tridoṣa (with Pitta and Kapha); corresponds to Air + Space (Dim 1).
coordinate.ontic_mode: Constituent — a doṣa (the bio-energetic principle of movement)
coordinate.key_relation: One of the tridoṣa (with Pitta and Kapha); corresponds to Air + Space (Dim 1).
coordinate.causal_status: Governing principle (governs all movement — circulation, breath, nerve impulse)

concepts.name: Part II: Pitta
concepts.gloss: Its five subdoshas—Pāchaka (governing digestion in the stomach), Rañjaka (governing liver metabolism and blood chemistry), Ālochaka (governing vision and the processing of light), Sādhaka (governing the emotional heart and the subtle transformation of feeling into wisdom), and Bhrājaka (governing skin pigmentation and the transformation of external stimuli)—together orchestrate the entire metabolic life of the organism. On the psychological plane, Pitta is the energy of intellect, ambition, courage, discrimination, and the focused will to achieve.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Physical–Mental (metabolism, digestion, discernment) · Ontic mode: Constituent — a doṣa (the bio-energetic principle of transformation) · Valence: Neutral faculty (balance is the aim) · Guṇa: Sattva–Rajas (illumination and intensity) · Scale: Individual — constitutional (prakṛti), current expression (vikṛti) varying · Causal status: Governing principle (governs transformation — digestion, metabolism, perception) · Key relation: One of the tridoṣa; corresponds to Fire (+ Water); the metabolic fire (agni).
coordinate.ontic_mode: Constituent — a doṣa (the bio-energetic principle of transformation)
coordinate.key_relation: One of the tridoṣa; corresponds to Fire (+ Water); the metabolic fire (agni).
coordinate.causal_status: Governing principle (governs transformation — digestion, metabolism, perception)

concepts.name: Part III: Kapha
concepts.gloss: Its five subdoshas—Kledaka (governing the mucous lining of the stomach), Avalambaka (governing the lungs, heart, and trachea), Bodhaka (governing saliva and taste), Tarpaka (governing cerebrospinal fluid and the “lubrication” of the brain), and Śleṣaka (governing joint fluid, connective tissue, and the sinovial membranes)—together orchestrate the entire structural and preservative life of the organism. On the psychological plane, Kapha is the energy of patience, loyalty, compassion, emotional stability, and the deep, enduring forms of love and memory.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Physical–Vital (structure, fluids, stability) · Ontic mode: Constituent — a doṣa (the bio-energetic principle of cohesion) · Valence: Neutral faculty (balance is the aim) · Guṇa: Tamas-dominant (stability, heaviness, cohesion) · Scale: Individual — constitutional (prakṛti), current expression (vikṛti) varying · Causal status: Governing principle (governs structure, lubrication, and immunity) · Key relation: One of the tridoṣa; corresponds to Earth + Water (Dim 1); the ground of stability.
coordinate.ontic_mode: Constituent — a doṣa (the bio-energetic principle of cohesion)
coordinate.key_relation: One of the tridoṣa; corresponds to Earth + Water (Dim 1); the ground of stability.
coordinate.causal_status: Governing principle (governs structure, lubrication, and immunity)

concepts.name: Dveṣa
concepts.gloss: Dveṣa is aversion that follows pain — the mirror image of rāga. It is the residue of past painful experience hardening into recoil: the dwelling on what hurt and the active avoidance of anything that resembles it. Where rāga reaches toward, dveṣa pushes away; together they form the two arms of the same grasping reflex.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital (aversion, recoil) · Ontic mode: Affliction — a kleśa (aversion) · Valence: Affliction (aversion to attenuate) · Guṇa: Rajas–Tamas · Scale: Individual (ādhyātmika) · Causal status: Branch (grows from Asmitā / Avidyā) · Key relation: Branch of Avidyā; antagonist-pair with Rāga; drives the unpleasant pole of Valence (Dim 7).
coordinate.key_relation: Branch of Avidyā; antagonist-pair with Rāga; drives the unpleasant pole of Valence (Dim 7).

concepts.name: Rāga
concepts.gloss: Rāga is attachment that follows pleasure — the dwelling on past enjoyments and the leaning-forward to repeat them. It is not the enjoyment itself, which is innocent, but the residue enjoyment leaves: a track laid down in memory that pulls the mind back toward the source and quietly organizes future behavior around getting more.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital (attraction, craving) · Ontic mode: Affliction — a kleśa (attachment) · Valence: Affliction (craving to attenuate) · Guṇa: Rajas-dominant · Scale: Individual (ādhyātmika) · Causal status: Branch (grows from Asmitā / Avidyā) · Key relation: Branch of Avidyā; antagonist-pair with Dveṣa; drives the pleasant pole of Valence (Dim 7).
coordinate.key_relation: Branch of Avidyā; antagonist-pair with Dveṣa; drives the pleasant pole of Valence (Dim 7).

concepts.name: Alabdha-bhūmikatva
concepts.gloss: Alabdha-bhumikatva is the inability to reach the higher states of yoga despite practice. There is effort but no fruit—failure to gain ground.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental–Psychic (the plateau) · Ontic mode: Obstacle — an antarāya (failure to gain ground) · Valence: Affliction / obstacle · Guṇa: Rajas–Tamas · Scale: Individual (ādhyātmika) · Causal status: Obstruction (stalled progress) · Key relation: One of the nine antarāyas; the felt failure to advance through the stages (Dim 11).
coordinate.ontic_mode: Obstacle — an antarāya (failure to gain ground)
coordinate.key_relation: One of the nine antarāyas; the felt failure to advance through the stages (Dim 11).

concepts.name: Ālasya
concepts.gloss: Alasya is physical and mental laziness—lack of effort, sloth, postponing practice. It is a tamasic obstacle related to but distinct from Styana (mental dullness specifically).

coordinate.raw: Seat: Physical–Vital (inertia, disinclination to effort) · Ontic mode: Obstacle — an antarāya (sloth) · Valence: Affliction / obstacle · Guṇa: Tamas-dominant · Scale: Individual (ādhyātmika) · Causal status: Obstruction (inertia that resists effort) · Key relation: One of the nine antarāyas; same as Ālasya the wave (Dim 9); kin to Styāna.
coordinate.key_relation: One of the nine antarāyas; same as Ālasya the wave (Dim 9); kin to Styāna.

concepts.name: Anavasthitatva
concepts.gloss: Anavasthitatva is the inability to maintain a state once attained—instability where ground is gained then lost. The mind cannot stabilize in higher states.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental–Psychic (loss of a gained level) · Ontic mode: Obstacle — an antarāya (instability) · Valence: Affliction / obstacle · Guṇa: Rajas-dominant · Scale: Individual (ādhyātmika) · Causal status: Obstruction (the loss of an attained ground) · Key relation: One of the nine antarāyas; counterpart to Alabdha-bhūmikatva — losing rather than failing to reach.
coordinate.key_relation: One of the nine antarāyas; counterpart to Alabdha-bhūmikatva — losing rather than failing to reach.
coordinate.causal_status: Obstruction (the loss of an attained ground)

concepts.name: Avirati
concepts.gloss: Avirati is craving for sense objects—the inability to turn away from sensory pleasures. It pulls the mind away from yoga practice toward sensory engagement.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital (sensual pull) · Ontic mode: Obstacle — an antarāya (sensual craving / non-restraint) · Valence: Affliction / obstacle · Guṇa: Rajas-dominant · Scale: Individual (ādhyātmika) · Causal status: Obstruction (craving that pulls attention outward) · Key relation: One of the nine antarāyas; kin to Rāga (Dim 19) and Kāma-vāsanā (Dim 12); antagonist to Brahmacharya.
coordinate.key_relation: One of the nine antarāyas; kin to Rāga (Dim 19) and Kāma-vāsanā (Dim 12); antagonist to Brahmacharya.

concepts.name: Bhrāntidarśana
concepts.gloss: Bhrantidarshana is false perception or erroneous view—wrong understanding of practice, philosophy, or experience. Mistaking progress, misidentifying experiences.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental (mistaken understanding) · Ontic mode: Obstacle — an antarāya (false perception) · Valence: Affliction / obstacle · Guṇa: Tamas–Rajas · Scale: Individual (ādhyātmika) · Causal status: Obstruction (wrong view that misdirects practice) · Key relation: One of the nine antarāyas; kin to Viparyaya (Dim 20) and Avidyā (Dim 19).
coordinate.key_relation: One of the nine antarāyas; kin to Viparyaya (Dim 20) and Avidyā (Dim 19).

concepts.name: Pramāda
concepts.gloss: Pramada is carelessness or negligence—lack of awareness and attention in practice. It is a moment-to-moment forgetting of yogic awareness.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental–Vital (heedlessness) · Ontic mode: Obstacle — an antarāya (carelessness) · Valence: Affliction / obstacle · Guṇa: Tamas–Rajas · Scale: Individual (ādhyātmika) · Causal status: Obstruction (inattention that lets practice lapse) · Key relation: One of the nine antarāyas; antagonist to the vigilance of mindful attention.
coordinate.key_relation: One of the nine antarāyas; antagonist to the vigilance of mindful attention.

concepts.name: Saṁśaya
concepts.gloss: Samshaya is doubt that undermines practice. Not healthy questioning, but corrosive doubt that prevents commitment and progress on the path.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental (wavering conviction) · Ontic mode: Obstacle — an antarāya (corrosive doubt) · Valence: Affliction / obstacle · Guṇa: Rajas–Tamas · Scale: Individual (ādhyātmika) · Causal status: Obstruction (doubt that undermines commitment) · Key relation: One of the nine antarāyas; the corrosive form of Vitarka’s deliberation (Dim 9); dissolved by resolve and faith.
coordinate.key_relation: One of the nine antarāyas; the corrosive form of Vitarka’s deliberation (Dim 9); dissolved by resolve and faith.

concepts.name: Styāna
concepts.gloss: Styana is mental dullness or apathy—a state of mental sluggishness where there is no enthusiasm for practice. It is a tamasic obstruction.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental (heaviness of mind) · Ontic mode: Obstacle — an antarāya (mental dullness) · Valence: Affliction / obstacle · Guṇa: Tamas-dominant · Scale: Individual (ādhyātmika) · Causal status: Obstruction (dullness that saps the will to practice) · Key relation: One of the nine antarāyas; kin to Ālasya and the tamasic vṛttis.
coordinate.key_relation: One of the nine antarāyas; kin to Ālasya and the tamasic vṛttis.
coordinate.causal_status: Obstruction (dullness that saps the will to practice)

concepts.name: Vyādhi
concepts.gloss: Vyadhi is disease or illness—the first of nine obstacles Patanjali names that distract the mind from yoga. Physical illness disturbs mental focus and impedes practice.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Physical (bodily distress) · Ontic mode: Obstacle — an antarāya (disease) · Valence: Affliction / obstacle (to remedy) · Guṇa: Tamas-tinged · Scale: Individual, with environmental cause · Causal status: Obstruction (bodily distress that stalls practice) · Key relation: One of the nine antarāyas; same as Vyādhi the wave (Dim 9); undermines the Annamaya ground.
coordinate.key_relation: One of the nine antarāyas; same as Vyādhi the wave (Dim 9); undermines the Annamaya ground.

concepts.name: Karuṇā
concepts.gloss: Karuna is compassion toward those who are suffering. It dissolves cruelty and indifference, creating mental purity and serenity.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital (tenderness toward pain) · Ontic mode: Virtue — a brahmavihāra (compassion) · Valence: Virtue to cultivate (near enemy: pity / grief; far enemy: cruelty) · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Interpersonal (Ādhibhautika) · Causal status: Condition (opens the heart to another’s suffering) · Key relation: One of the four brahmavihāras — the key for those who suffer; the ripened form of Śoka’s Karuṇā Rasa (Dim 8).
coordinate.valence: Virtue to cultivate (near enemy: pity / grief; far enemy: cruelty)
coordinate.key_relation: One of the four brahmavihāras — the key for those who suffer; the ripened form of Śoka’s Karuṇā Rasa (Dim 8).
coordinate.causal_status: Condition (opens the heart to another’s suffering)

concepts.name: Maitrī
concepts.gloss: Maitri is friendliness or loving-kindness toward those who are happy and successful. It dissolves envy and creates serene mental clarity. One of the four locks/keys to mental peace.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital (the heart’s warmth) · Ontic mode: Virtue — a brahmavihāra (loving-kindness) · Valence: Virtue to cultivate (near enemy: attachment; far enemy: ill-will) · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Interpersonal (Ādhibhautika) · Causal status: Condition (warms the heart toward all beings) · Key relation: One of the four brahmavihāras — the key for those who are happy; antidote to Dveṣa (Dim 19).
coordinate.valence: Virtue to cultivate (near enemy: attachment; far enemy: ill-will)
coordinate.key_relation: One of the four brahmavihāras — the key for those who are happy; antidote to Dveṣa (Dim 19).
coordinate.causal_status: Condition (warms the heart toward all beings)

concepts.name: Muditā
concepts.gloss: Mudita is joy at others’ virtue or happiness, particularly the gladness at seeing meritorious actions. It dissolves competitive envy regarding spiritual or moral accomplishment.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital (gladness at another’s good) · Ontic mode: Virtue — a brahmavihāra (sympathetic joy) · Valence: Virtue to cultivate (near enemy: comparison / flattery; far enemy: envy) · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Interpersonal (Ādhibhautika) · Causal status: Condition (rejoices in another’s good fortune) · Key relation: One of the four brahmavihāras — the key for the fortunate and virtuous; antidote to Asūyā (envy, Dim 9).
coordinate.valence: Virtue to cultivate (near enemy: comparison / flattery; far enemy: envy)
coordinate.key_relation: One of the four brahmavihāras — the key for the fortunate and virtuous; antidote to Asūyā (envy, Dim 9).

concepts.name: Upekṣā
concepts.gloss: Upeksha is equanimity (sometimes translated as ‘indifference’) toward those who are unvirtuous or wicked. Not approval, but freedom from being disturbed by their vice. It preserves mental peace.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Psychic–Mental (the steady, even heart) · Ontic mode: Virtue — a brahmavihāra (equanimity) · Valence: Virtue to cultivate (near enemy: indifference; far enemy: agitation / partiality) · Guṇa: Sattva, tending beyond the guṇas · Scale: Interpersonal (Ādhibhautika), grounded in the individual · Causal status: Condition (holds the heart even amid what cannot be changed) · Key relation: One of the four brahmavihāras — the key for the difficult and beyond-help; kin to Śama (Dim 8) and Udāsīnatā (Dim 10).
coordinate.valence: Virtue to cultivate (near enemy: indifference; far enemy: agitation / partiality)
coordinate.key_relation: One of the four brahmavihāras — the key for the difficult and beyond-help; kin to Śama (Dim 8) and Udāsīnatā (Dim 10).
coordinate.causal_status: Condition (holds the heart even amid what cannot be changed)

concepts.name: Abhayam
concepts.gloss: Abhayam is the absence of fear—not the absence of awareness of danger, but the absence of psychological fear that paralyzes action. It is the first divine quality listed in Chapter 16 and considered foundational for spiritual growth.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Psychic · Ontic mode: Virtue — a daivī quality (fearlessness) · Valence: Virtue to cultivate · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual (ādhyātmika) · Causal status: Foundation (the first divine quality, ground of the rest) · Key relation: First of the daivī sampat; antagonist to Bhaya (Dim 8); rooted in Self-knowledge.
coordinate.key_relation: First of the daivī sampat; antagonist to Bhaya (Dim 8); rooted in Self-knowledge.
coordinate.causal_status: Foundation (the first divine quality, ground of the rest)

concepts.name: Ahiṁsā
concepts.gloss: Ahimsa is the principle of non-harm and non-violence, extending to all living beings. It encompasses harmlessness in thought, word, and deed, and reflects respect for all life.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Mental · Ontic mode: Virtue — a daivī quality (non-harming) · Valence: Virtue to cultivate (the foundational restraint) · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Interpersonal (Ādhibhautika) · Causal status: Foundation (the root of the ethical life) · Key relation: A daivī quality; same as Ahiṁsā the yama (Dim 11), the ground of all restraints.
coordinate.key_relation: A daivī quality; same as Ahiṁsā the yama (Dim 11), the ground of all restraints.
coordinate.causal_status: Foundation (the root of the ethical life)

concepts.name: Akrodha
concepts.gloss: Akrodha is the absence of destructive anger. It does not mean the absence of all righteous indignation, but freedom from the kind of anger that distorts perception, harms others, and degrades consciousness. It is one of the divine qualities.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital · Ontic mode: Virtue — a daivī quality (freedom from anger) · Valence: Virtue to cultivate · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Interpersonal (Ādhibhautika) · Causal status: Condition (non-reactivity to provocation) · Key relation: A daivī quality; the direct antagonist to Krodha (its āsurī counterpart, 4.2).
coordinate.ontic_mode: Virtue — a daivī quality (freedom from anger)
coordinate.key_relation: A daivī quality; the direct antagonist to Krodha (its āsurī counterpart, 4.2).

concepts.name: Damaḥ
concepts.gloss: Damah is the disciplined control of the senses—the ability to direct attention and engagement of sight, hearing, taste, touch, and smell consciously rather than being driven by sensory cravings.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Physical–Vital · Ontic mode: Virtue — a daivī quality (self-control) · Valence: Virtue to cultivate · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual (ādhyātmika) · Causal status: Condition (mastery of the sense-organs) · Key relation: A daivī quality; same as Dama the treasure (Dim 23); kin to Pratyāhāra (Dim 11).
coordinate.key_relation: A daivī quality; same as Dama the treasure (Dim 23); kin to Pratyāhāra (Dim 11).

concepts.name: Dambha
concepts.gloss: Dambha is hypocrisy—the false display of virtues one does not actually possess. It is putting on a religious or moral facade for show, while inner reality contradicts the outer presentation.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Mental · Ontic mode: Affliction — an āsurī quality (hypocrisy / ostentation) · Valence: Affliction (to see through and drop) · Guṇa: Rajas–Tamas · Scale: Interpersonal (Ādhibhautika) · Causal status: Distortion (a false show of virtue) · Key relation: An āsurī quality; antagonist to Hrī (modesty) and Satya (truthfulness).
coordinate.key_relation: An āsurī quality; antagonist to Hrī (modesty) and Satya (truthfulness).

concepts.name: Dayā
concepts.gloss: Daya is the active quality of compassion—feeling for others’ suffering and being moved to alleviate it. It is one of the divine qualities and reflects recognition of the same Self in all beings.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital · Ontic mode: Virtue — a daivī quality (compassion) · Valence: Virtue to cultivate · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Interpersonal (Ādhibhautika) · Causal status: Condition (tenderness toward all beings) · Key relation: A daivī quality; kin to Karuṇā (Dim 22).
coordinate.key_relation: A daivī quality; kin to Karuṇā (Dim 22).

concepts.name: Hrī
concepts.gloss: Hrih is the natural sense of modesty and shame regarding inappropriate or unworthy actions. It is the inner moral compass that signals when conduct violates dharma, creating spontaneous restraint.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital–Mental · Ontic mode: Virtue — a daivī quality (modesty / moral conscience) · Valence: Virtue to cultivate · Guṇa: Sattva · Scale: Interpersonal (Ādhibhautika) · Causal status: Condition (the healthy conscience that recoils from wrong) · Key relation: A daivī quality; the wholesome form of Vrīḍā (Dim 9); antagonist to Dambha (4.1).
coordinate.ontic_mode: Virtue — a daivī quality (modesty / moral conscience)
coordinate.key_relation: A daivī quality; the wholesome form of Vrīḍā (Dim 9); antagonist to Dambha (4.1).
coordinate.causal_status: Condition (the healthy conscience that recoils from wrong)

concepts.name: Kāma
concepts.gloss: Kama is desire, longing, and the drive for pleasure and gratification. It is a natural force that motivates action but can also lead to suffering if uncontrolled. Kama includes sensory pleasures and emotional desires.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital · Ontic mode: Affliction — an āsurī quality (binding, insatiable desire) · Valence: Affliction (to transmute) · Guṇa: Rajas-dominant · Scale: Individual (ādhyātmika) · Causal status: Root distortion (the insatiable craving the Gītā names, with Krodha, the gate of hell) · Key relation: An āsurī quality; kin to Rāga (Dim 19) and Kāma-vāsanā (Dim 12); root of the demonic trajectory.
coordinate.ontic_mode: Affliction — an āsurī quality (binding, insatiable desire)
coordinate.key_relation: An āsurī quality; kin to Rāga (Dim 19) and Kāma-vāsanā (Dim 12); root of the demonic trajectory.
coordinate.causal_status: Root distortion (the insatiable craving the Gītā names, with Krodha, the gate of hell)

concepts.name: Krodha
concepts.gloss: Krodha is anger that arises when desires are frustrated. The Gita describes the chain: contemplation of objects → attachment → desire → anger when desire is thwarted → delusion → loss of memory → loss of discrimination → destruction.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital · Ontic mode: Affliction — an āsurī quality (anger) · Valence: Affliction (to transmute) · Guṇa: Rajas-dominant · Scale: Interpersonal (Ādhibhautika) · Causal status: Distortion (destructive reactivity) · Key relation: An āsurī quality; the same Krodha as the sthāyibhāva (Dim 8), here as a binding endowment; antagonist to Akrodha (3.3).
coordinate.key_relation: An āsurī quality; the same Krodha as the sthāyibhāva (Dim 8), here as a binding endowment; antagonist to Akrodha (3.3).

concepts.name: Mārdavam
concepts.gloss: Märdavam is gentleness—softness in conduct, manner, and approach. It is the quality of being kind in interaction with others, not harsh or rough. It reflects refinement and compassion in expression.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital · Ontic mode: Virtue — a daivī quality (gentleness / softness) · Valence: Virtue to cultivate · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Interpersonal (Ādhibhautika) · Causal status: Condition (a soft, non-harsh disposition) · Key relation: A daivī quality; direct antagonist to Pāruṣyam (harshness, 4.3).
coordinate.key_relation: A daivī quality; direct antagonist to Pāruṣyam (harshness, 4.3).

concepts.name: Pāruṣyam
concepts.gloss: Pärusyam is harshness, particularly in speech but also in manner. It is the rough, hurtful, cruel way of communicating that damages others and reflects an inner state of aggression.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Vital · Ontic mode: Affliction — an āsurī quality (harshness of speech and manner) · Valence: Affliction (to transmute) · Guṇa: Rajas–Tamas · Scale: Interpersonal (Ādhibhautika) · Causal status: Distortion (cutting, cruel expression) · Key relation: An āsurī quality; kin to Ugratā (Dim 9); direct antagonist to Mārdavam (gentleness, 3.6).
coordinate.ontic_mode: Affliction — an āsurī quality (harshness of speech and manner)
coordinate.key_relation: An āsurī quality; kin to Ugratā (Dim 9); direct antagonist to Mārdavam (gentleness, 3.6).

concepts.name: Satya
concepts.gloss: Satya is truthfulness in thought, speech, and action. It is alignment with what is real and right, expressed with love (Satyam priyahitamchayat — truth spoken with love or be silent). It includes both factual truth and the deeper truth of Self.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Mental–Vital · Ontic mode: Virtue — a daivī quality (truthfulness) · Valence: Virtue to cultivate · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Interpersonal (Ādhibhautika) · Causal status: Condition (alignment of word, thought, and deed) · Key relation: A daivī quality; same as Satya the yama (Dim 11); kin to Ārjavam (Dim 10).
coordinate.key_relation: A daivī quality; same as Satya the yama (Dim 11); kin to Ārjavam (Dim 10).
coordinate.causal_status: Condition (alignment of word, thought, and deed)

concepts.name: Shaucham
concepts.gloss: Shaucham is purity—both external (physical cleanliness, hygiene) and internal (purity of mind, motives, emotions). It is the foundation that allows clarity and spiritual progress.

coordinate.raw: Seat: Physical–Mental · Ontic mode: Virtue — a daivī quality (purity, inner and outer) · Valence: Virtue to cultivate · Guṇa: Sattva-dominant · Scale: Individual (ādhyātmika) · Causal status: Condition (cleanliness of body and mind) · Key relation: A daivī quality; same as Śaucha the niyama (Dim 11).
coordinate.ontic_mode: Virtue — a daivī quality (purity, inner and outer)
coordinate.key_relation: A daivī quality; same as Śaucha the niyama (Dim 11).
